title: HealthCheck timer tries to execute HealthCheck when container is paused
body:
### Issue Description

When the container with the HealtCheck timer is paused. The timer is not paused and still attempts to perform a `podman healthcheck run`. 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. `cid=$(podman run -dt --replace --name hc1 --health-interval 5s --healthcheck-command 'echo test' quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest)`
2. `podman pause hc1`
3.  Wait a few seconds (6s)
4. `systemctl --user status ${cid}-\*.{service,timer}`

### Describe the results you received

The timer runs even after the container is paused. Systemctl displays an error:
![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f1f87c2f-675f-4320-9793-1179bfd48cd6)


### Describe the results you expected

The timer should be stopped or removed when the container is paused. If the container is unpaused, the timers should be started or recreated. The correct behavior could be the subject of discussion.

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

This was found [here](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24442#discussion_r1831681089).

comments.body: I don't think this needs to be fixed urgently, but it does feel like a bug - consuming CPU unnecessarily and spamming the logs for something that Podman knows about and can handle sensible.

FYI, we use a transient timer so there stopping == removing for these. So basically `podman pause/unpause` would need to the the same as `podman start/stop` regarding the timer. Although the one problem would be that the interval is no longer kept properly, each unpause would trigger the unit right away currently but that should be a edge case that can be ignored IMO.

And I agree it is not urgent given no user reported this so far.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@Luap99  I have checked the `podman stop/start`, and it works properly.
